stimulus: Researchers Maya Lindqvist and Olivia Carr studied scooter-sharing behavior in Stockholm, Sweden. They asked adult riders about their experience, reasons for riding, and route preferences. The researchers claim that experienced riders who mainly ride scooters to commute tend to prefer dedicated bike paths over scooter-share-friendly streets.
stem: Which survey response from a scooter rider in Stockholm would best support the underlined claim?

answerOptions.content: “I just downloaded the scooter-share app last week, and I’m looking forward to trying it on weekends.”

“I ride a shared scooter to my office every weekday. There is a wider road I could take, but I always use the dedicated bike path through the riverside park because I feel safer with no cars near me.”
“I usually scooter to work, but I’m taking the metro this week because the weather has been too rainy for scootering.”
“I sometimes use a scooter to go meet friends after dinner. I don’t really pay attention to which roads I take.”

rationale:
Choice B is correct because the rider is both experienced (rides every weekday), commutes (to office), and explicitly prefers the dedicated bike path over the wider road — exactly the combination described in the underlined claim.

Choice A is incorrect: a new user riding for fun isn’t an experienced commuter. Choice C is incorrect: although the rider is a commuter, the response doesn’t address route preferences. Choice D is incorrect: this rider isn’t commuting and doesn’t prefer particular routes.
